components.name: hooks
components.description: React hooks that wire together the REPL's runtime behavior -- from input handling and keybindings to IDE integration, voice mode, background task navigation, and notification scheduling.

components.name: skills
components.description: The skill system that discovers and loads SKILL.md files from project directories and bundled sources, builds tool-like wrappers for MCP-provided skills, and manages the skill registry.

components.name: tasks
components.description: Task type implementations for background work: local shell commands, local agent subprocesses, remote agent sessions, in-process teammates, dream tasks, and workflow script execution.

hooks.name: useAfterFirstRender
hooks.description: Executes a callback after the first Ink render cycle completes, used for initialization that depends on the terminal being fully mounted.

hooks.name: useApiKeyVerification
hooks.description: Validates the configured API key on startup and surfaces authentication errors as banner notifications when the key is missing, invalid, or expired.

hooks.name: useArrowKeyHistory
hooks.description: Enables up/down arrow key navigation through command history in the prompt input, matching shell-like behavior for recalling previous messages.

hooks.name: useAssistantHistory
hooks.description: Tracks and provides access to the conversation's assistant message history, enabling features that need to reference or navigate past model responses.

hooks.name: useAwaySummary
hooks.description: Triggers the away-summary generation when a user returns to a session after a period of inactivity, displaying a brief recap of recent context.

hooks.name: useBackgroundTaskNavigation
hooks.description: Handles keyboard navigation between background task panels, allowing users to cycle through and inspect running sub-agents and shell tasks.

hooks.name: useBlink
hooks.description: A simple timer hook that toggles a boolean value at a fixed interval, used for cursor blinking and other pulsing UI indicators.

hooks.name: useCanUseTool
hooks.description: The permission evaluation hook that determines whether a specific tool invocation is allowed based on the current permission mode, user rules, and policy limits, returning allow/deny/ask decisions.

hooks.name: useCancelRequest
hooks.description: Handles Ctrl+C behavior during active API requests, managing the abort signal lifecycle and distinguishing between canceling the current request vs. exiting.

hooks.name: useChromeExtensionNotification
hooks.description: Displays a notification prompting users to install or update the Chrome extension when the Claude-in-Chrome integration would be beneficial.

hooks.name: useClaudeCodeHintRecommendation
hooks.description: Shows contextual hints recommending Claude Code features that the user might not know about, based on their usage patterns.

hooks.name: useClipboardImageHint
hooks.description: Detects when an image is in the clipboard and shows a hint about pasting images into the conversation for visual analysis.

hooks.name: useCommandKeybindings
hooks.description: Binds keyboard shortcuts to slash commands, enabling direct key combinations (like Ctrl+K for /compact) without typing the command.

hooks.name: useCommandQueue
hooks.description: Manages a queue of commands to be executed sequentially, ensuring that queued slash commands don't overlap or interfere with each other.

hooks.name: useCopyOnSelect
hooks.description: Automatically copies selected text to the clipboard when the user makes a selection in the terminal output.

hooks.name: useDeferredHookMessages
hooks.description: Collects and delivers system messages from lifecycle hooks that fire during turns, deferring their display until an appropriate point in the conversation flow.

hooks.name: useDiffData
hooks.description: Computes and caches diff data for files changed during the session, providing the data model behind the /diff command and diff-related UI components.

hooks.name: useDiffInIDE
hooks.description: Opens file diffs in the connected IDE when available, leveraging the IDE integration to show richer diff views than the terminal can provide.

hooks.name: useDirectConnect
hooks.description: Manages the direct WebSocket connection to an external client, handling the lifecycle of the direct-connect server for IDE and remote control integration.

hooks.name: useDoublePress
hooks.description: Detects double-press of a key within a time window, used for gestures like double-Escape to force-exit or double-Enter to submit.

hooks.name: useDynamicConfig
hooks.description: Polls for and applies dynamic configuration changes from the server, enabling runtime feature flag updates and A/B test assignment without restarting.

hooks.name: useElapsedTime
hooks.description: Tracks elapsed time since a reference point (typically request start), providing a ticking counter for the progress display during model responses.

hooks.name: useExitOnCtrlCD
hooks.description: Implements the Ctrl+C/Ctrl+D exit behavior in the base input handler, distinguishing between interrupt and EOF signals for graceful shutdown.

hooks.name: useExitOnCtrlCDWithKeybindings
hooks.description: Extended exit handler that respects custom keybinding overrides for Ctrl+C and Ctrl+D, ensuring the customized bindings don't break exit behavior.

hooks.name: useFileHistorySnapshotInit
hooks.description: Initializes the file history snapshot system on session start, creating the baseline snapshot that enables /rewind to restore files to their pre-session state.

hooks.name: useGlobalKeybindings
hooks.description: Registers and dispatches global keyboard shortcuts that work regardless of which component has focus, providing application-wide hotkey support.

hooks.name: useHistorySearch
hooks.description: Implements Ctrl+R reverse history search in the input, filtering past commands as the user types a search pattern.

hooks.name: useIDEIntegration
hooks.description: Orchestrates the full IDE integration lifecycle including MCP server discovery, connection management, file sync, and bidirectional RPC between Claude Code and the connected editor.

hooks.name: useIdeAtMentioned
hooks.description: Detects when the user @-mentions a file from the IDE's active editor context, automatically resolving the file path for inclusion in the conversation.

hooks.name: useIdeConnectionStatus
hooks.description: Monitors and reports the connection status of the IDE integration, surfacing connection/disconnection events as UI indicators.

hooks.name: useIdeLogging
hooks.description: Forwards Claude Code log events to the connected IDE's output channel, providing visibility into agent behavior from within the editor.

hooks.name: useIdeSelection
hooks.description: Captures text selection events from the connected IDE and makes them available as context for the current conversation turn.

hooks.name: useInboxPoller
hooks.description: Periodically polls for incoming messages in the session inbox (from remote sessions, teammates, or bridge connections), delivering them to the appropriate handlers.

hooks.name: useInputBuffer
hooks.description: Manages the text input buffer with cursor position tracking, supporting insertion, deletion, and cursor movement operations for the REPL prompt.

hooks.name: useIssueFlagBanner
hooks.description: Displays a banner when there's an active issue flag on the session, alerting the user to known problems or degraded service conditions.

hooks.name: useLogMessages
hooks.description: Captures and surfaces log messages from internal systems as visible notifications in the terminal UI, bridging the gap between debug logs and user-visible output.

hooks.name: useLspPluginRecommendation
hooks.description: Recommends LSP-related plugins when the user's project could benefit from language server features that aren't currently configured.

hooks.name: useMailboxBridge
hooks.description: Bridges the mailbox messaging system with the React component tree, routing inter-component messages through the centralized mailbox context.

hooks.name: useMainLoopModel
hooks.description: Tracks and provides the currently active model for the main conversation loop, handling model switches and ensuring components react to model changes.

hooks.name: useManagePlugins
hooks.description: Monitors plugin state changes and manages the plugin lifecycle within the React component tree, handling installation, updates, and removal events.

hooks.name: useMemoryUsage
hooks.description: Monitors JavaScript heap memory usage and surfaces warnings when memory consumption approaches dangerous levels, helping detect memory leaks.

hooks.name: useMergedClients
hooks.description: Combines API clients from multiple sources (built-in, plugin-provided, MCP) into a unified client interface that the query engine can use transparently.

hooks.name: useMergedCommands
hooks.description: Merges commands from all sources (built-in, plugins, skills) into a single command registry, resolving naming conflicts and providing the complete command list.

hooks.name: useMergedTools
hooks.description: Assembles the complete tool set by merging built-in tools, MCP-provided tools, plugin tools, and feature-gated tools into the unified tool array passed to the model.

hooks.name: useMinDisplayTime
hooks.description: Ensures UI elements (like progress indicators) stay visible for a minimum duration even if the underlying operation completes quickly, preventing visual flicker.

hooks.name: useNotifyAfterTimeout
hooks.description: Sends a system notification when the model's response takes longer than a configurable threshold, alerting the user that a long-running operation is still in progress.

hooks.name: useOfficialMarketplaceNotification
hooks.description: Displays a notification about the official plugin marketplace when the user might benefit from discovering available extensions.

hooks.name: usePasteHandler
hooks.description: Intercepts paste events to detect and handle large text pastes and image pastes, converting them to the appropriate message format with content references.

hooks.name: usePluginRecommendationBase
hooks.description: Base hook that evaluates plugin recommendation rules and manages the recommendation state, powering the various plugin recommendation notification hooks.

hooks.name: usePrStatus
hooks.description: Monitors the status of GitHub pull requests related to the current session, displaying CI check results and review states.

hooks.name: usePromptSuggestion
hooks.description: Feeds the PromptSuggestion service's output into the UI, showing contextual suggestions below the input prompt based on conversation state.

hooks.name: usePromptsFromClaudeInChrome
hooks.description: Receives prompts forwarded from the Claude-in-Chrome extension, enabling users to send tasks from their browser to the CLI session.

hooks.name: useQueueProcessor
hooks.description: Processes the command queue by dequeuing and executing commands one at a time, managing the sequential execution contract for queued operations.

hooks.name: useRemoteSession
hooks.description: Manages the lifecycle of a remote session connection, handling authentication, message routing, and reconnection for teleported sessions.

hooks.name: useReplBridge
hooks.description: Establishes and manages the REPL bridge connection that enables external control of the REPL from IDE extensions and other surfaces.

hooks.name: useSSHSession
hooks.description: Detects and handles SSH session characteristics, adjusting terminal behavior and feature availability when Claude Code is running over an SSH connection.

hooks.name: useScheduledTasks
hooks.description: Monitors and triggers scheduled (cron) tasks at their configured intervals, managing the cron scheduler lifecycle within the React component tree.

hooks.name: useSearchInput
hooks.description: Implements the search-mode input handler for Ctrl+R history search, managing search state, result filtering, and navigation between matches.

hooks.name: useSessionBackgrounding
hooks.description: Handles session backgrounding events (terminal losing focus, SSH disconnects) by pausing non-essential work and preparing for potential resume.

hooks.name: useSettings
hooks.description: Loads and provides reactive access to the full settings state, triggering re-renders when settings change through any source (config files, commands, remote sync).

hooks.name: useSettingsChange
hooks.description: Detects and responds to settings file changes (from external edits or other sessions), applying updates to the running session in real time.

hooks.name: useSkillImprovementSurvey
hooks.description: Occasionally prompts users with a survey about skill quality after skill invocations, collecting feedback to improve skill effectiveness.

hooks.name: useSkillsChange
hooks.description: Monitors the filesystem for changes to SKILL.md files and reloads the skill registry when skills are added, modified, or removed.

hooks.name: useSwarmInitialization
hooks.description: Initializes the multi-agent swarm infrastructure when swarm mode is enabled, setting up the messaging channels and agent registry.

hooks.name: useSwarmPermissionPoller
hooks.description: Polls for pending permission requests from agents in the swarm, surfacing them to the user for approval when teammates need elevated access.

hooks.name: useTaskListWatcher
hooks.description: Watches the background task list for state changes (task completion, failure, new output) and triggers UI updates when task status changes.

hooks.name: useTasksV2
hooks.description: The next-generation task management hook that handles the full lifecycle of background tasks including creation, monitoring, output streaming, and cleanup.

hooks.name: useTeammateViewAutoExit
hooks.description: Automatically exits the teammate detail view when the observed teammate terminates, returning the user to the main conversation view.

hooks.name: useTeleportResume
hooks.description: Handles resuming a session after teleporting back from a remote environment, reconciling local and remote state and restoring the conversation context.

hooks.name: useTerminalSize
hooks.description: Tracks terminal dimensions and triggers re-renders when the terminal is resized, providing responsive layout information to all components.

hooks.name: useTextInput
hooks.description: The core text input hook that handles character insertion, cursor movement, line editing, history navigation, and paste events for the REPL prompt.

hooks.name: useTimeout
hooks.description: A declarative timeout hook that calls a callback after a delay, automatically cleaning up on unmount -- the React-idiomatic alternative to setTimeout.

hooks.name: useTurnDiffs
hooks.description: Computes per-turn file diffs by comparing file states before and after each assistant turn, powering the inline diff display in the conversation output.

hooks.name: useTypeahead
hooks.description: Provides typeahead/autocomplete suggestions in the input prompt, drawing from file paths, command names, agent names, and MCP resources.

hooks.name: useUpdateNotification
hooks.description: Checks for available Claude Code updates and displays a notification when a new version is available, with update action options.

hooks.name: useVimInput
hooks.description: Replaces the standard text input handler with a Vim-emulation layer when Vim mode is active, routing keystrokes through the Vim motion/operator state machine.

hooks.name: useVirtualScroll
hooks.description: Implements virtual scrolling for long content areas, rendering only the visible portion of large outputs to maintain terminal performance.

hooks.name: useVoice
hooks.description: The primary voice mode hook that coordinates push-to-talk recording, speech-to-text transcription, and audio submission into the conversation flow.

hooks.name: useVoiceEnabled
hooks.description: Evaluates all preconditions for voice mode availability (feature flags, auth, platform support) and provides a reactive enabled/disabled status.

hooks.name: useVoiceIntegration
hooks.description: Wires the voice recording and transcription pipeline into the React component tree, managing the UI state transitions between listening, transcribing, and submitting.
